title: Co-environmentalization Dynamics
one_sentence: This module extends authority editing into coupled environments and installs bidirectional ontology-axiology separation as a safety boundary.
core_thesis: In co-environmentalization, authority editing becomes coupled governance, and trace strength and value openness must be separated in both directions.

core_compression.CE: two systems become each other's persistence boundary conditions
core_compression.coupled_authority: authority edits in X re-enter Y and authority edits in Y re-enter X
core_compression.reality_anchor: externality not reproducible by internal perturbation alone
core_compression.value_openness: V_o = future relational openness / maintainable coupling
core_compression.safety_boundary: S_e != V_o; S_e does not imply V_o; V_o does not imply S_e
core_compression.bidirectional_trace_value_separation: Trace does not prove value; value does not guarantee trace.

diagnostic_regimes.coupled_drift: tau amplifies but editing fails to stabilize
diagnostic_regimes.coupled_closure: one-sided non-editability propagates toward closed attractor
diagnostic_regimes.coupled_open_persistence: non-resonance is treated as editing material and authority remains revisable on both sides

negative_constraints: Not proof that impact equals value.
Not claim that coupling is always good.
Do not conflate trace strength S_e with value openness V_o.
Classify coupled authority and ontology-axiology separation dynamics only.
Do not infer value from trace strength.
Do not infer trace strength from value openness.

natural_language: Strong existence trace does not guarantee value.
Even the most sublime value does not guarantee strong existence trace.

prevents.value_romanticism: sublime value assumed to guarantee survival, propagation, registration, or strong trace
